title: Name and read whole numbers in the millions
blurb: Use place value grouping of ones, tens and hundreds to name numbers in the millions.

title: Order numbers in the millions
blurb: Arrange large whole numbers in ascending and descending order.

title: Round whole numbers to a given place value
blurb: Round large numbers to the nearest hundred, thousand, million, etc.

title: Partition, regroup and rename numbers to 1 billion
blurb: Express numbers in standard and non-standard partitioned forms up to 1 billion.

title: Express and interpret thousandths as decimals
blurb: Read and write decimals to 3 places and the place value of each digit.

title: Partition decimals using place value
blurb: Break a decimal into the sum of its place-value parts, e.g. 5.37 = 5 + 0.3 + 0.07.

title: Compare and order decimals up to 3 decimal places
blurb: Order decimals and interpret trailing zeros, e.g. 0.170 = 0.17.

title: Scale decimals by 10, 100, 1/10 and 1/100
blurb: Find numbers 10 or 100 times, or 1/10 or 1/100 of, a given decimal.

title: Approximate decimals to a given number of places
blurb: Round a decimal to a stated number of decimal places.

title: Place decimals on a number line
blurb: Locate decimals of up to 3 places on a number line.

title: Locate integers on a number line
blurb: Place positive and negative whole numbers in relation to zero.

title: Interpret integers in everyday contexts
blurb: Use negative numbers in contexts such as temperature.

title: Understand percentages as parts per 100
blurb: Recognise that % means per 100 and 100% is the whole.

title: Recall equivalent percentages, decimals and fractions
blurb: Connect benchmark values such as 1/2, 1/4, 3/4 with their decimals and percentages.

title: Find 10% of a quantity
blurb: Recognise 10% as one-tenth and use it to find 10% of an amount.

title: Find 10%, 25% and 50% of a quantity
blurb: Equate 10% to ÷10, 25% to ÷4, and 50% to halving.

title: Calculate a sale price after a discount
blurb: Find the price of an item after a 10%, 25% or 50% discount.

title: Solve addition and subtraction word problems
blurb: Interpret and solve worded addition and subtraction problems, including multistep.

title: Apply mental strategies for addition and subtraction
blurb: Use levelling, constant difference, addition for subtraction and bridging.

title: Add and subtract numbers with different numbers of digits
blurb: Use place value to add or subtract 3 or more numbers.

title: Round numbers to estimate a calculation
blurb: Round numbers appropriately to obtain an estimate.

title: Check reasonableness using estimation
blurb: Use estimation and place value to judge whether an answer is reasonable.

title: Solve multistep problems with more than one operation
blurb: Choose and combine operations to solve multistep word problems.

title: Add and subtract decimals
blurb: Add and subtract decimals up to 3 decimal places.

title: Solve word problems adding and subtracting decimals
blurb: Apply decimal addition and subtraction to worded problems.

title: Find the product of numbers
blurb: Use the term product and multiply 2 or more numbers.

title: Determine the factors of a whole number
blurb: List the factors of a number and express it as products.

title: Identify prime and composite numbers
blurb: Determine whether a number is prime, composite or neither.

title: Multiply by 10, 100, 1000 and their multiples
blurb: Use place value to multiply one-digit numbers by powers of 10 and their multiples.

title: Estimate the product of two numbers
blurb: Use multiples of 10 or 100 to estimate a product.

title: Multiply using the area model and distributive property
blurb: Partition by place value to multiply, e.g. 684×5 = (600×5)+(80×5)+(4×5).

title: Multiply by a one-digit number using the algorithm
blurb: Record multiplication by a one-digit number with a formal algorithm.

title: Factorise numbers to aid mental multiplication
blurb: Use the associative property, e.g. 12×25 = 3×4×25 = 3×100.

title: Multiply by 2-digit numbers
blurb: Multiply 2- and 3-digit numbers by 2-digit numbers using the area model and algorithm.

title: Solve multiplication word problems
blurb: Interpret and solve worded multiplication problems.

title: Model division with remainders
blurb: Use arrays or diagrams to model division where there is a remainder.

title: Use fact families to solve division with remainders
blurb: Apply known multiplication facts to divide, e.g. 27÷6 = 4 remainder 3.

title: Connect division and multiplication
blurb: Relate divisor, quotient and remainder, e.g. 25 = 4×6 + 1.

title: Estimate the result of a division
blurb: Estimate quotients when dividing by a one-digit divisor.

title: Divide large numbers by a one-digit divisor
blurb: Partition by place value and use known multiples to divide, e.g. 3248÷4.

title: Interpret remainders in division problems
blurb: Decide how to use or round a remainder in a worded problem.

title: Check reasonableness of multiplication and division
blurb: Use estimation to judge multiplication and division answers.

title: Select efficient multiplication and division strategies
blurb: Choose mental or written methods for numbers up to 4 digits by 1- and 2-digit numbers.

title: Solve rate problems with multiplication and division
blurb: Use multiplication and division to solve worded rate problems.

title: Multiply decimals by single-digit numbers
blurb: Use mental strategies to multiply benchmark decimals, e.g. 3.5×2.

title: Multiply and divide decimals by powers of 10
blurb: Shift place value to multiply or divide a decimal by 10, 100 or 1000.

title: Estimate the product of a decimal and a whole number
blurb: Estimate to judge the magnitude of a decimal multiplication.

title: Complete number sentences to find missing numbers
blurb: Find unknowns in number sentences with more than one operation.

title: Use inverse operations to solve number sentences
blurb: Rearrange a number sentence using inverse operations to find an unknown.

title: Record division as a fraction
blurb: Recognise that a÷b can be written as the fraction a/b.

title: Build a table of values from a geometric pattern
blurb: Create a table of values for a pattern involving multiples.

title: Describe a pattern of multiples in words
blurb: Describe a pattern in terms of multiplication rather than addition.

title: Find the rule relating quantities in a table
blurb: Determine the rule connecting the two rows of a table of values.

title: Apply the order of operations
blurb: Evaluate number sentences applying the agreed order of operations.

title: Use brackets to control the order of operations
blurb: Insert and interpret grouping symbols () to make a number sentence true.

title: Recognise fractions need a common whole
blurb: Understand that fractions must refer to the same whole to be compared or added.

title: Compare unit fractions to 1/2
blurb: Decide whether a unit fraction is smaller or larger than the benchmark 1/2.

title: Compare and order unit fractions
blurb: Order unit fractions (denominators 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10) on a number line.

title: Add and subtract fractions with the same denominator
blurb: Combine and find the difference of fractions sharing a denominator.

title: Subtract a fraction from a whole number
blurb: Use the complement principle, e.g. 1 − 1/3 = 2/3.

title: Solve word problems with same-denominator fractions
blurb: Apply same-denominator addition and subtraction to worded problems.

title: Interpret a fraction as a division
blurb: Read a fraction as numerator divided by denominator, e.g. 3/4 = 3÷4.

title: Order fractions with related denominators
blurb: Order common fractions with related denominators using diagrams and number lines.

title: Create and record equivalent fractions
blurb: Generate equivalent fractions using diagrams, words and notation, e.g. 6/8 = 3/4.

title: Compare fractions using area and discrete models
blurb: Represent and compare fractions of shapes and collections.

title: Reconstruct the whole from a fractional part
blurb: Given a non-unit fractional part, find the whole quantity.

title: Add and subtract fractions with related denominators
blurb: Use equivalence to add and subtract fractions like 1/4 and 1/8.

title: Find a unit fraction of a quantity
blurb: Find 1/2, 1/4, 1/5 and 1/10 of a measure or collection.

title: Find a non-unit fraction of a whole number
blurb: Use a tape diagram to find fractions like 2/5 of 30.

title: Solve word problems involving a fraction of a quantity
blurb: Apply fractions of quantities to worded problems, expressing remainders as decimals.

title: Plot points in the first quadrant
blurb: Plot and label points given coordinates, horizontal position first.

title: Read coordinates in the first quadrant
blurb: Identify and record the coordinates of given points.

title: Use metres and kilometres
blurb: Recognise 1000 m = 1 km and record distances in km.

title: Estimate lengths and distances
blurb: Choose an appropriate unit and estimate lengths and distances.

title: Calculate the perimeter of 2D shapes
blurb: Find the perimeter of squares, rectangles and triangles.

title: Determine which sides are needed for a perimeter
blurb: Decide which side lengths are required to calculate a perimeter.

title: Measure angles with a protractor
blurb: Measure angles up to 360° and record them using the degree symbol (°).

title: Estimate angles using benchmarks
blurb: Estimate the size of an angle using known angles such as 90°.

title: Construct angles with a protractor
blurb: Draw angles of up to 360° using a protractor.

title: Classify angles by size
blurb: Identify acute, right, obtuse, straight, reflex angles and revolutions.

title: Plot and read points in all four quadrants
blurb: Plot and record coordinates across all four quadrants of the number plane.

title: Describe coordinate changes under transformation
blurb: Describe how coordinates change when a point is translated or reflected across an axis.

title: Record lengths using decimal notation
blurb: Connect whole-number and decimal length, e.g. 165 cm = 1.65 m.

title: Convert between metres and kilometres
blurb: Use the decimal place value system to convert length units.

title: Compare lengths by converting to a common unit
blurb: Convert measurements to the same unit before comparing.

title: Compare perimeters of rectangles with the same area
blurb: Investigate how rectangles with equal area can have different perimeters.

title: Solve length and perimeter problems
blurb: Solve problems involving length and perimeter, including different units.

title: Recognise angle relationships in diagrams
blurb: Identify right angles, angles on a straight line and angles at a point.

title: Recognise perpendicular lines
blurb: Recognise that perpendicular lines intersect at right angles (90°).

title: Find adjacent angles that add to 90°
blurb: Use the fact that adjacent angles forming a right angle add to 90°.

title: Find adjacent angles on a straight line
blurb: Use the fact that angles on a straight line add to 180°.

title: Find angles at a point
blurb: Use the fact that angles at a point add to 360°.

title: Classify triangles
blurb: Identify equilateral, isosceles and scalene triangles.

title: Classify quadrilaterals by their properties
blurb: Compare side and angle properties of squares, rectangles, parallelograms and rhombuses.

title: Investigate symmetry of quadrilaterals
blurb: Identify line and rotational symmetry of quadrilaterals.

title: Identify regular and irregular polygons
blurb: Distinguish regular from irregular polygons.

title: Use hectares and square kilometres
blurb: Recognise when to use ha and km² and that 10 000 m² = 1 ha.

title: Calculate the area of a rectangle
blurb: Find area as length × width in cm², m² and km².

title: Relate area, perimeter and dimensions of rectangles
blurb: Recognise rectangles with the same area or perimeter can have different dimensions.

title: Describe transformations of shapes
blurb: Use the terms translate, reflect and rotate to describe transformations.

title: Dissect and rearrange shapes
blurb: Cut and rearrange one shape to form another, preserving size.

title: Find the area of composite figures
blurb: Split or rearrange an L-shaped figure to find its area.

title: Find the area of a parallelogram
blurb: Transform a parallelogram into a rectangle to find area = base × height.

title: Find the area of a triangle
blurb: Find a triangle's area as half that of a parallelogram with the same base and height.

title: Compare prisms and pyramids
blurb: Compare properties such as uniform cross-section, bases and apex.

title: Name prisms and pyramids
blurb: Name a prism or pyramid by the shape of its base.

title: Sketch 3D objects from different views
blurb: Visualise and sketch top, front and side views of 3D objects.

title: Work with nets of 3D objects
blurb: Identify, sketch and name prisms and pyramids from their nets.

title: Choose units for capacity
blurb: Select appropriate units (mL, L) to measure capacity.

title: Compare volumes using displacement
blurb: Compare volumes by observing change in water level.

title: Record capacities using decimal notation
blurb: Connect whole-number and decimal capacity, e.g. 375 mL = 0.375 L.

title: Construct prisms and pyramids
blurb: Build skeletal and solid models of prisms and pyramids from drawings.

title: Use the cubic metre
blurb: Estimate and measure larger volumes in cubic metres.

title: Describe volume as layers of cubes
blurb: Relate cubes in one layer and the number of layers to find volume.

title: Calculate the volume of a rectangular prism
blurb: Find volume in cm³ and m³ from its dimensions.

title: Relate volume and dimensions of prisms
blurb: Recognise prisms with the same volume can have different dimensions.

title: Choose units and devices for mass
blurb: Select appropriate units and devices to measure mass (g, kg, t).

title: Record masses using decimal notation
blurb: Connect whole-number and decimal mass, e.g. 3 kg 250 g = 3.25 kg.

title: Read 24-hour time
blurb: Read and use 24-hour time language.

title: Convert between 12- and 24-hour time
blurb: Convert times between am/pm and 24-hour notation.

title: Read and interpret timetables
blurb: Use timetables involving 12- and 24-hour time.

title: Convert between units of mass
blurb: Convert between grams, kilograms and tonnes.

title: Solve mass problems with different units
blurb: Solve problems involving masses given in different units.

title: Calculate elapsed time
blurb: Find the elapsed time between start and finish times.

title: Represent time intervals as decimals
blurb: Express time intervals as decimals, e.g. 2 h 15 min = 2.25 h.

title: Solve duration problems
blurb: Solve duration problems in 12- and 24-hour notation.

title: Design a survey to collect data
blurb: Pose and refine questions to collect categorical or numerical data.

title: Collect categorical and numerical data
blurb: Collect data by observation or survey.

title: Tabulate collected data
blurb: Organise collected data into tables.

title: Choose an appropriate data display and scale
blurb: Select a suitable display and a horizontal and vertical scale for the data.

title: Construct column graphs with a many-to-one scale
blurb: Draw column graphs using a many-to-one scale.

title: Draw a timeline to scale
blurb: Draw an accurate timeline using a many-to-one scale.

title: Interpret line graphs
blurb: Read line graphs using the scales on the axes.

title: Interpret tables, column graphs and line graphs
blurb: Describe and interpret data in tables and graphs, including total values.

title: Interpret side-by-side column graphs
blurb: Read side-by-side column graphs for two categorical variables.

title: Compare data displays by range and mode
blurb: Compare distributions by their range and most frequent value (mode).

title: Interpret data in media and texts
blurb: Interpret data representations found in digital media and factual texts.

title: Identify misleading data representations
blurb: Spot bias and misleading displays such as broken axes.

title: List equally likely outcomes
blurb: Identify and record all equally likely outcomes of a chance experiment.

title: Represent probabilities as fractions
blurb: Express the probability of an equally likely outcome as a fraction.

title: Establish that probabilities sum to 1
blurb: Recognise that the probabilities of all outcomes add to one.

title: Distinguish frequency from probability
blurb: Separate the frequency of an outcome from its probability.

title: Compare observed and expected frequencies
blurb: Compare expected and observed frequencies, including unequal outcomes.

title: Create random generators
blurb: Build spinners or bags to follow specified probabilities or proportions.

title: Assign probabilities to unequal outcomes
blurb: Assign fraction probabilities to outcomes that are not equally likely.

title: Assign probabilities using benchmark equivalents
blurb: Use benchmark fractions, decimals and percentages to describe likelihood.

title: Compare probabilities over many trials
blurb: Compare expected and observed probabilities over small and large numbers of trials.

title: Estimate a collection by sampling with replacement
blurb: Estimate the makeup of a collection by repeated sampling with replacement.

title: Compare and order integers
blurb: Compare integers using < and >, and order them on a number line.

title: Add and subtract positive and negative integers
blurb: Add and subtract directed numbers, using a number line where helpful.

title: Model situations with directed number sentences
blurb: Construct directed number sentences to model real contexts.

title: Multiply and divide positive and negative integers
blurb: Multiply and divide directed numbers and determine the sign of the result.

title: Apply the order of operations to integers
blurb: Evaluate expressions with integers, grouping symbols and the order of operations.

title: Find the highest common factor (HCF)
blurb: Determine the HCF of two whole numbers.

title: Simplify fractions
blurb: Simplify fractions using the HCF or repeated common factors.

title: Compare and order fractions with different denominators
blurb: Use equivalence to compare and order fractions with unrelated denominators.

title: Use approximation notation
blurb: Apply the ≈ symbol and judge when an approximation is appropriate.

title: Use recurring decimal notation
blurb: Write repeating decimals using dot or vinculum notation.

title: Classify decimals as terminating or recurring
blurb: Decide whether a fraction gives a terminating or recurring decimal.

title: Define and identify rational numbers
blurb: Recognise rational numbers as those expressible as a/b with integer a, b and b≠0.

title: Convert between fractions, decimals and percentages
blurb: Convert flexibly between fraction, decimal (terminating/recurring) and percentage forms.

title: Convert between improper fractions and mixed numbers
blurb: Represent improper fractions as mixed numbers and decimals, and vice versa.

title: Identify and define irrational numbers
blurb: Recognise numbers such as √2 and π that cannot be written as a/b.

title: Locate irrational numbers on a number line
blurb: Find the approximate position of irrational numbers on a number line.

title: Order fractions, decimals and percentages
blurb: Compare and order positive and negative fractions, decimals and percentages.

title: Add and subtract fractions and mixed numbers
blurb: Add and subtract fractions and mixed numbers using a common denominator.

title: Multiply and divide decimals
blurb: Multiply and divide decimals, with and without digital tools.

title: Multiply and divide fractions and mixed numbers
blurb: Multiply and divide fractions and mixed numbers.

title: Generalise multiplying or dividing by a number between 0 and 1
blurb: Explain why multiplying by a value between 0 and 1 makes a number smaller.

title: Solve problems with fractions and decimals of quantities
blurb: Apply multiplication and division of fractions and decimals to solve problems.

title: Express one quantity as a fraction or percentage of another
blurb: Represent one quantity as a fraction, decimal or percentage of another.

title: Find a percentage of a quantity
blurb: Calculate any percentage of a quantity.

title: Calculate percentage increases and decreases
blurb: Find the result of increasing or decreasing a quantity by a percentage.

title: Use multipliers for percentage change
blurb: Use multipliers such as ×1.05 or ×0.87 for percentage increase or decrease.

title: Solve percentage problems with the unitary method
blurb: Use the unitary method to solve real-life percentage problems.

title: Solve financial percentage problems
blurb: Solve problems involving GST, profit and loss as percentages.

title: Use ratios to compare quantities
blurb: Compare two or more quantities measured in the same units using a ratio.

title: Express a ratio part as a fraction of the whole
blurb: Identify one part of a ratio as a fraction of the total.

title: Simplify ratios
blurb: Simplify a ratio to its lowest whole-number terms.

title: Apply the unitary method to ratio problems
blurb: Find the value of one part to solve ratio problems.

title: Divide a quantity in a given ratio
blurb: Share a quantity according to a given ratio.

title: Solve real-life ratio problems
blurb: Solve ratio problems including recipes and map scales.

title: Distinguish ratios from rates
blurb: Explain that ratios compare like units and rates compare different units.

title: Represent information as a simplified rate
blurb: Express given information as a simplified rate.

title: Convert between units for rates
blurb: Convert rates such as km/h to m/s and vice versa.

title: Solve problems involving rates
blurb: Solve real-life rate problems, including best buys.

title: Interpret distance–time graphs
blurb: Interpret distance–time graphs made of straight-line segments.

title: Calculate speed from a distance–time graph
blurb: Find the speed of straight-line segments of a distance–time graph.

title: Construct distance–time graphs
blurb: Draw distance–time graphs made of straight-line segments from data.

title: Understand pronumerals and variables
blurb: Recognise that pronumerals represent numbers and may vary.

title: Use algebraic notation conventions
blurb: Apply conventions for multiplication, division and powers in algebra.

title: Translate between words and algebraic expressions
blurb: Generate algebraic expressions from descriptions and vice versa.

title: Substitute values into algebraic expressions
blurb: Replace pronumerals with numbers and evaluate the result.

title: Generate a number pattern from an expression
blurb: Build a number pattern by substituting values into an expression.

title: Collect like terms
blurb: Identify like terms and add or subtract them to simplify expressions.

title: Multiply and divide algebraic terms
blurb: Simplify products, quotients and simple algebraic fractions.

title: Expand brackets using the distributive law
blurb: Expand and simplify expressions by removing grouping symbols.

title: List the factors of an algebraic term
blurb: Identify the numerical and algebraic factors of a single term.

title: Factorise by taking out the HCF
blurb: Factorise expressions by extracting the highest common factor.

title: Describe numbers in index form
blurb: Use base, index, power and exponent to describe numbers in index form.

title: Evaluate numbers in index notation
blurb: Write numbers in expanded form and evaluate powers, including powers of 10.

title: Order of operations with indices
blurb: Apply the order of operations to expressions involving indices.

title: Apply divisibility tests
blurb: Use divisibility tests for 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 and 10.

title: Express a number as a product of prime factors
blurb: Write a whole number as a product of primes using index notation.

title: Use square roots and cube roots
blurb: Use root notation and the relationship between squares/cubes and their roots.

title: Estimate square and cube roots
blurb: Estimate roots of non-square and non-cube numbers between known roots.

title: Verify √(ab) = √a × √b
blurb: Verify the product rule for square roots using numerical examples.

title: Order of operations with roots and powers
blurb: Evaluate expressions involving square roots, cube roots and powers.

title: Establish the index laws
blurb: Establish the multiplication, division and power-of-a-power index laws.

title: Establish and use the zero index
blurb: Establish that a⁰ = 1 and apply it.

title: Apply index laws with numerical bases
blurb: Simplify and evaluate expressions with numerical bases using index laws.

title: Distinguish expressions from equations
blurb: Compare algebraic expressions and equations.

title: Solve 2-step linear equations
blurb: Solve linear equations of up to 2 steps, including pronumerals on both sides.

title: Model word problems with equations
blurb: Set up and solve equations from worded problems.

title: Verify equation solutions by substitution
blurb: Check a solution by substituting it back into the equation.

title: Solve equations from formulas
blurb: Solve linear equations arising from substituting values into formulas.

title: Reason about two solutions of x² = c
blurb: Explain why x²=c has two solutions when c>0 and none when c<0.

title: Solve quadratic equations of the form ax² = c
blurb: Solve ax²=c giving exact and decimal-approximation answers.

title: Solve quadratics from formulas
blurb: Solve quadratic equations arising from substituting into a formula.

title: Plot points on the Cartesian plane
blurb: Plot and identify points, including non-integer coordinates, in all quadrants.

title: Generate an equation from a pattern
blurb: Describe a number pattern in words and write its equation.

title: Move between representations of a linear relationship
blurb: Connect pattern, equation, table, coordinates and graph of a linear relationship.

title: Apply an equation from a pattern
blurb: Use a pattern's equation to find values for larger or smaller numbers.

title: Graph a linear relationship
blurb: Graph a straight line from its equation and compare multiple lines.

title: Solve real-life linear relationship problems
blurb: Describe and solve real-life problems modelled by linear relationships.

title: Recognise points that satisfy a line
blurb: Verify that a point lies on a line by satisfying its equation.

title: Solve a linear equation graphically
blurb: Use the graph of a line to solve a corresponding linear equation.

title: Find the intersection of two lines
blurb: Graph two lines and identify and verify their point of intersection.

title: Find the perimeter of composite figures
blurb: Solve problems involving the perimeter of composite plane figures.

title: Identify the features of circles
blurb: Name the radius, diameter, arc, chord, sector, segment and tangent.

title: Define π
blurb: Define π as the ratio of circumference to diameter of any circle.

title: Find the circumference of a circle
blurb: Apply C = πd or 2πr to solve problems.

title: Find arc lengths and perimeters of sectors
blurb: Find arc lengths and the perimeter of sectors, quadrants and semicircles.

title: Identify the hypotenuse
blurb: Identify the hypotenuse as the side opposite the right angle.

title: State Pythagoras' theorem
blurb: Express Pythagoras' theorem algebraically and in words.

title: Find a side using Pythagoras' theorem
blurb: Apply Pythagoras' theorem to find an unknown side, in exact or decimal form.

title: Apply the converse of Pythagoras' theorem
blurb: Test whether a triangle is right-angled using the converse.

title: Solve problems with Pythagoras' theorem
blurb: Solve practical problems and identify Pythagorean triads.

title: Find the area of a circle
blurb: Apply A = πr² to find the area of a circle.

title: Find the area of sectors, quadrants and semicircles
blurb: Find the area of sectors, quadrants and semicircles.

title: Find areas of composites involving circle parts
blurb: Calculate areas of composite shapes involving quadrants, semicircles and sectors.

title: Find the area of a kite or rhombus
blurb: Apply A = ½xy using the diagonals of a kite or rhombus.

title: Find the area of a trapezium
blurb: Apply A = (h/2)(a+b) to find the area of a trapezium.

title: Find areas of composites with trapeziums, kites and rhombuses
blurb: Calculate areas of composite shapes involving trapeziums, kites and rhombuses.

title: Choose appropriate units of area
blurb: Select an appropriate unit to measure a given area.

title: Convert between units of area
blurb: Convert between mm², cm², m², ha and km².

title: Draw different views of prisms
blurb: Represent prisms and prism combinations from top, side, front and back views.

title: Identify cross-sections of prisms
blurb: Identify cross-sections and recognise uniform cross-sections of prisms.

title: Find the volume of a prism
blurb: Apply V = Ah to find the volume of a prism with uniform cross-section.

title: Find the volume of a cylinder
blurb: Apply V = πr²h to find the volume of a cylinder.

title: Use units of volume and capacity
blurb: Recognise and use units including kL and ML.

title: Convert between units of volume and capacity
blurb: Convert between mm³, cm³, m³, mL, L, kL and ML.

title: Solve volume and capacity problems
blurb: Solve practical problems involving prisms and cylinders.

title: Use geometry language and notation
blurb: Name and label points, lines, intervals, rays and angles by convention.

title: Identify angle pairs at a point
blurb: Identify vertically opposite, complementary, supplementary and adjacent angles.

title: Notate parallel and perpendicular lines
blurb: Use symbols and conventions for parallel and perpendicular lines and transversals.

title: Identify transversal angle pairs
blurb: Identify corresponding, alternate and co-interior angle pairs.

title: Use parallel-line angle properties
blurb: Use that corresponding and alternate angles are equal and co-interior angles are supplementary.

title: Find unknown angles at a point
blurb: Find unknown angles at a point and give reasons.

title: Find unknown angles with parallel lines
blurb: Find unknown angles involving parallel lines and give reasons.

title: Describe properties of special quadrilaterals
blurb: Verify and describe side, angle and diagonal properties of special quadrilaterals.

title: Distinguish convex and non-convex quadrilaterals
blurb: Distinguish convex from non-convex (concave) quadrilaterals.

title: Apply the angle sum of a triangle
blurb: Prove and apply that the interior angles of a triangle sum to 180°.

title: Apply the exterior angle of a triangle
blurb: Prove and use that an exterior angle equals the sum of the two opposite interior angles.

title: Apply the angle sum of a quadrilateral
blurb: Apply that the interior angles of a quadrilateral sum to 360°.

title: Find unknown sides and angles in figures
blurb: Apply triangle and quadrilateral properties to find unknowns, giving reasons.

title: Define a statistical variable
blurb: Define a variable as a characteristic that can be measured or counted.

title: Classify variables as numerical or categorical
blurb: Classify and describe variables as numerical or categorical.

title: Describe data subtypes
blurb: Describe numerical data as discrete/continuous and categorical data as nominal/ordinal.

title: Represent data with a variety of graphs
blurb: Represent single datasets using histograms, dot plots, stem-and-leaf plots and more.

title: Apply graph conventions
blurb: Include sources, titles, labels and scales when displaying data.

title: Choose and justify a graph type
blurb: Select the most suitable graph for a dataset and justify the choice.

title: Interpret graphs to draw conclusions
blurb: Interpret data displays to draw conclusions and make predictions.

title: Explain misleading graphs
blurb: Explain why a graphical representation can lead to misinterpretation.

title: Calculate mean, median, mode and range
blurb: Calculate the mean, median, mode and range of a dataset.

title: Classify measures of centre
blurb: Classify the mean, median and mode as measures of centre.

title: Describe the modality of a dataset
blurb: Describe datasets as uniform, unimodal, bimodal or multimodal.

title: Compare datasets using measures
blurb: Compare datasets using mean, median, mode and range.

title: Identify clusters, gaps and outliers
blurb: Informally identify clusters, gaps and outliers in datasets.

title: Explain effects on measures of centre
blurb: Explain how adding/removing values and outliers affect centre and range.

title: Choose the most appropriate measure of centre
blurb: Determine and justify the best measure of centre in context.

title: Describe the shape of a distribution
blurb: Describe distributions as symmetrical, negatively skewed or positively skewed.

title: Relate skew to measures of centre
blurb: Associate skew with the relative size of the mean, median and mode.

title: Distinguish a census from a sample
blurb: Define and distinguish a census and a sample.

title: Draw conclusions from data
blurb: Draw conclusions from data collected by census or sampling.

title: List the sample space
blurb: List the sample space for a chance experiment.

title: Calculate theoretical probability
blurb: Find P(event) = favourable ÷ total outcomes for equally likely outcomes.

title: Understand the range of probabilities
blurb: Recognise probabilities range from 0 to 1 and that all outcomes sum to 1.

title: Determine observed probability
blurb: Determine observed (experimental) probability as relative frequency from trials.

title: Identify the complement of an event
blurb: Identify and describe the complement of an event.

title: Find probabilities of complementary events
blurb: Use P(event) + P(complement) = 1 to solve problems.

title: Calculate earnings from work
blurb: Calculate wages, overtime, penalty rates, commission, piecework and leave loading.

title: Calculate taxable income and net pay
blurb: Determine taxable income, PAYG tax and net earnings after deductions.

title: Calculate simple interest
blurb: Use I = Prn to solve simple interest problems algebraically and graphically.

title: Calculate costs of spending options
blurb: Compare buying on terms, buy-now-pay-later and short-term loans.

title: Calculate compound interest by repeated multiplication
blurb: Find compound interest over a few periods using repeated multiplication.

title: Apply the compound interest formula
blurb: Use FV = PV(1 + r)ⁿ to solve compound interest problems.

title: Solve depreciation problems
blurb: Use S = V₀(1 − r)ⁿ to solve depreciation problems.

title: Compare simple and compound interest
blurb: Compare simple and compound interest in practical situations.

title: Simplify algebraic fractions with numerical denominators
blurb: Add, subtract, multiply and divide algebraic fractions with numerical denominators.

title: Expand binomial products
blurb: Expand products such as (x+3)(x+8) using the distributive law or an area model.

title: Apply index laws to algebraic terms
blurb: Apply the index laws to variables with positive-integer indices.

title: Simplify algebraic expressions with the zero index
blurb: Establish x⁰ = 1 algebraically and simplify expressions involving it.

title: Simplify algebraic products and quotients
blurb: Simplify powers, products and quotients of algebraic terms using index laws.

title: Apply negative-integer indices
blurb: Establish and evaluate negative-integer indices for numerical bases.

title: Solve 3-step linear equations
blurb: Solve linear equations of up to 3 steps, including grouping symbols and pronumerals on both sides.

title: Solve linear equations with one algebraic fraction
blurb: Solve linear equations involving one algebraic fraction using up to 3 steps.

title: Find the gradient of an interval
blurb: Use m = rise/run, including zero and undefined gradients.

title: Find the midpoint of an interval
blurb: Find the midpoint of an interval by averaging the coordinates.

title: Find the distance between two points
blurb: Use Pythagoras' theorem to find the length of an interval on the Cartesian plane.

title: Identify x- and y-intercepts of a line
blurb: Find the x- and y-intercepts of a line and test if a point lies on it.

title: Relate parallel lines to equal gradients
blurb: Explain that parallel lines have equal gradients.

title: Graph horizontal and vertical lines
blurb: Recognise and graph y = c and x = k, and the axes y = 0 and x = 0.

title: Interpret gradient–intercept form
blurb: Interpret m as the gradient and c as the y-intercept in y = mx + c.

title: Find a line's equation from gradient and intercept
blurb: Write y = mx + c given the gradient and y-intercept.

title: Graph a line using gradient and intercept
blurb: Graph y = mx + c using the gradient and y-intercept.

title: Find a line's equation from its graph
blurb: Determine the gradient and y-intercept from a graph to find its equation.

title: Find equations of parallel and perpendicular lines
blurb: Use equal gradients (parallel) and gradient product −1 (perpendicular) to find equations.

title: Graph quadratics and exponentials from tables
blurb: Use tables of values and technology to graph quadratics and exponentials.

title: Identify parabolas and exponential curves
blurb: Identify the graphs and equations of parabolas and exponential curves.

title: Graph parabolas and identify their features
blurb: Graph y = kx² + c and identify vertex, intercepts, axis of symmetry and concavity.

title: Graph exponentials and identify their features
blurb: Graph y = aˣ and describe the y-intercept and asymptote.

title: Distinguish linear, quadratic and exponential graphs
blurb: Associate straight lines, parabolas and exponential curves with their equations.

title: Apply non-linear relationships in context
blurb: Recognise non-linear relationships in context and solve simultaneous equations graphically.

title: Identify measurement prefixes
blurb: Describe prefixes such as milli, centi and kilo for very small and large units.

title: Find the absolute error of a measurement
blurb: Find the precision and absolute error (½ × precision) of an instrument.

title: Calculate percentage error
blurb: Calculate percentage error = absolute error ÷ measurement × 100%.

title: Round to significant figures
blurb: Round numbers to a specified number of significant figures.

title: Express numbers in scientific notation
blurb: Write very large and very small numbers in scientific notation and as decimals.

title: Order numbers in scientific notation
blurb: Order numbers expressed in scientific notation.

title: Calculate with scientific notation
blurb: Estimate and calculate with scientific notation using index laws.

title: Label sides of a right-angled triangle
blurb: Label the hypotenuse, opposite and adjacent sides relative to an angle.

title: Define the trigonometric ratios
blurb: Define and identify the sine, cosine and tangent ratios.

title: Explain the constancy of trig ratios
blurb: Use similar triangles to explain why trig ratios are constant for a given angle.

title: Find an angle from a trig ratio
blurb: Find the size of an angle given one of its trigonometric ratios.

title: Find an unknown side using trigonometry
blurb: Apply trig ratios to find unknown side lengths in right-angled triangles.

title: Find an unknown angle using trigonometry
blurb: Apply trig ratios to find unknown angles, including in degrees and minutes.

title: Solve practical trigonometry problems
blurb: Solve practical right-angled triangle problems using trigonometry.

title: Solve elevation and depression problems
blurb: Identify and solve problems involving angles of elevation and depression.

title: Interpret and convert bearings
blurb: Interpret and convert between true and compass bearings.

title: Solve bearings problems
blurb: Solve practical problems involving bearings using trigonometry.

title: Solve composite area problems
blurb: Solve practical problems involving the areas of composite shapes.

title: Identify and create nets of prisms
blurb: Identify faces and edges and create nets of right prisms.

title: Find the surface area of a prism
blurb: Find the surface area of prisms from their nets, excluding curved surfaces.

title: Find the surface area of a cylinder
blurb: Develop and apply A = 2πr² + 2πrh for closed cylinders.

title: Find surface areas of composite solids
blurb: Solve problems involving surface areas of cylinders and composite solids.

title: Find volumes of composite prisms
blurb: Find volumes of composite right prisms dissected into triangles and quadrilaterals.

title: Find volumes of prisms with curved cross-sections
blurb: Find volumes of prisms with sector, semicircle or quadrant cross-sections.

title: Find volumes of composite solids
blurb: Calculate volumes of composite solids of prisms and cylinders and solve practical problems.

title: Describe properties of similar figures
blurb: Recognise equal angles and proportional sides in similar figures.

title: Write similarity statements
blurb: Name similar figures with matching vertices and match corresponding parts.

title: Determine and apply scale factors
blurb: Determine scale factors and use them to enlarge or reduce figures.

title: Find unknown sides in similar figures
blurb: Use the scale factor to find unknown side lengths in similar figures.

title: Solve scale drawing problems
blurb: Solve problems involving scale drawings, maps and plans.

title: Calculate and compare standard deviation
blurb: Calculate standard deviation using digital tools and compare datasets.

title: Determine the 5-number summary
blurb: Find the minimum, quartiles, median and maximum of a dataset.

title: Determine the interquartile range
blurb: Find the IQR and compare the relative merits of range and IQR.

title: Represent data using box plots
blurb: Construct a box plot from the 5-number summary.

title: Compare datasets using box plots
blurb: Compare datasets using parallel box plots and the 5-number summary.

title: Identify skewness from displays
blurb: Identify skewness or symmetry from histograms, dot plots and box plots.

title: Identify bivariate data
blurb: Distinguish 1-variable from bivariate data and association from causation.

title: Identify independent and dependent variables
blurb: Identify the independent and dependent variable in a relationship.

title: Represent bivariate data with a scatter plot
blurb: Gather and represent bivariate data using a scatter plot.

title: Draw a line of best fit
blurb: Draw a line of best fit by eye on a scatter plot.

title: Describe association in bivariate data
blurb: Describe association by form, strength and direction.

title: Interpolate and extrapolate from a line of best fit
blurb: Use the line of best fit to interpolate and extrapolate, noting limitations.

title: Distinguish independent and dependent events
blurb: Explain the difference between independent and dependent events and the effect of replacement.

title: Record outcomes for multistage experiments
blurb: Record all outcomes using tree diagrams and arrays.

title: Find probabilities of independent events
blurb: Use P(A and B) = P(A) × P(B) for multistage independent events.

title: Find probabilities of dependent events
blurb: Find probabilities of multistage dependent events (without replacement).

title: Use complements in multistage experiments
blurb: Associate complementary events with probabilities in multistage experiments.

title: Design and use probability simulations
blurb: Design, conduct and evaluate simulations to model probabilities.

title: Describe and represent direct variation
blurb: Recognise direct proportion y∝x or y=kx and identify the constant of variation.

title: Describe and represent inverse variation
blurb: Recognise inverse proportion y∝1/x, y=k/x or xy=k and identify the constant of variation.

title: Recognise and graph direct variation
blurb: Identify y=kx as a straight line through the origin with gradient k and graph it.

title: Recognise inverse variation from graphs
blurb: Identify y=k/x as a curve (hyperbola).

title: Solve variation problems using an equation
blurb: Find the constant of variation and use the equation to solve direct or inverse variation problems.

title: Use linear conversion graphs
blurb: Convert between units using a linear conversion graph and interpret its slope.

title: Analyse graphs with a constant rate of change
blurb: Interpret the gradient of a straight-line graph as a constant rate of increase or decrease.

title: Interpret distance–time graphs with variable speed
blurb: Describe straight (constant) and curved (variable) segments of a distance–time graph.

title: Describe rates of change qualitatively
blurb: Use terms such as 'increasing at a decreasing rate' to describe curved graphs.

title: Construct graphs of variable rates of change
blurb: Represent a described variable rate of change of a quantity over time as a graph.

title: Simplify algebraic fractions involving indices
blurb: Simplify algebraic fractions such as 10a⁴/(5a²) and 9a²b/(3ab).

title: Operate with algebraic fractions with pronumerals in the denominator
blurb: Add, subtract, multiply and divide algebraic fractions with pronumerals and/or indices in the denominator.

title: Factorise by taking out a common algebraic factor
blurb: Factorise expressions, including indices and negative coefficients, using the HCF.

title: Factorise monic quadratic trinomials
blurb: Factorise x²+bx+c into two binomial factors.

title: Solve problems involving expansion and factorisation
blurb: Apply expansion and factorisation of algebraic expressions to solve problems.

title: Add and subtract algebraic fractions with binomial numerators
blurb: Add and subtract fractions such as (2x+5)/6 + (x−4)/3.

title: Prove and apply special products
blurb: Apply (a−b)(a+b)=a²−b² and (a±b)²=a²±2ab+b².

title: Expand and simplify expressions with special products
blurb: Expand and simplify combinations of binomial and special products.

title: Factorise using special products and grouping
blurb: Factorise the difference of two squares, by grouping in pairs, and monic and non-monic trinomials.

title: Simplify algebraic fractions by factorising
blurb: Simplify algebraic fractions using factorisation, e.g. (x²+3x+2)/(x+2).

title: Establish negative-integer indices
blurb: Establish a⁻ⁿ = 1/aⁿ for algebraic bases using index patterns and laws.

title: Convert between negative and positive indices
blurb: Rewrite expressions with negative-integer indices as positive-index expressions and vice versa.

title: Apply index laws with negative-integer indices
blurb: Simplify algebraic products and quotients involving negative-integer indices.

title: Simplify expressions of the form (a/b)⁻ⁿ
blurb: Use x⁻¹ as the reciprocal of x to simplify expressions such as (a/b)⁻ⁿ.

title: Describe real numbers
blurb: Describe real numbers as points on a number line, including both rationals and irrationals.

title: Convert recurring decimals to fractions
blurb: Convert between recurring decimals and their fractional form using digital tools.

title: Define and identify surds
blurb: Describe a surd as an irrational root ⁿ√x and recognise it as an exact value.

title: Determine when square roots are defined
blurb: Recognise √x is undefined for x<0, √0=0, and √x is the positive square root.

title: Apply the basic surd results
blurb: Use (√x)²=x=√(x²), √(xy)=√x·√y and √(x/y)=√x/√y for x,y>0.

title: Simplify expressions involving surds
blurb: Apply the four operations to simplify surd expressions.

title: Expand and simplify expressions involving surds
blurb: Expand products of surd binomials and simplify the result.

title: Rationalise the denominator of a surd
blurb: Rationalise denominators of the form a√b/(c√d).

title: Describe and use fractional indices
blurb: Apply a^(1/n)=ⁿ√a and a^(m/n)=ⁿ√(aᵐ).

title: Translate between surd and index form
blurb: Convert expressions between surd form and fractional-index form and evaluate them.

title: Solve monic quadratic equations by factorising
blurb: Solve x²+bx+c=0 using factorisation.

title: Solve cubic equations of the form ax³=k
blurb: Solve ax³=k, giving exact and decimal answers, recognising the unique solution.

title: Represent inequalities on a number line
blurb: Graph inequalities such as x<2, x≥−1 and −1<x≤3 on a number line.

title: Solve linear inequalities
blurb: Solve linear inequalities, reversing the sign when multiplying or dividing by a negative, and graph the solution.

title: Solve linear equations with two or more fractions
blurb: Solve linear equations of more than 3 steps involving two or more algebraic fractions.

title: Change the subject of a formula
blurb: Rearrange a literal equation to make a different variable the subject.

title: Solve quadratic equations by factorisation
blurb: Solve ax²+bx+c=0 by factorising, including non-monic quadratics.

title: Solve quadratics by completing the square
blurb: Solve ax²+bx+c=0 by completing the square.

title: Apply the quadratic formula
blurb: Use x=(−b±√(b²−4ac))/(2a) to solve quadratic equations.

title: Use the discriminant to determine the nature of roots
blurb: Evaluate b²−4ac to decide whether a quadratic has real, equal or no real solutions.

title: Model and solve word problems with quadratics
blurb: Set up and solve quadratic equations from contexts and formulas, verifying solutions.

title: Solve equations reducible to quadratics
blurb: Use substitution to solve equations such as x⁴−13x²+36=0.

title: Solve simultaneous equations graphically
blurb: Solve linear simultaneous equations by finding the point of intersection of their graphs.

title: Solve simultaneous equations algebraically
blurb: Solve linear simultaneous equations using substitution and elimination.

title: Model word problems with simultaneous equations
blurb: Set up and solve simultaneous equations from worded problems and interpret the solution.

title: Identify identities and contradictions
blurb: Relate identities to coincident lines and contradictions to parallel lines.

title: Apply the midpoint formula
blurb: Use M=((x₁+x₂)/2, (y₁+y₂)/2) to find the midpoint of an interval.

title: Apply the gradient formula
blurb: Use m=(y₂−y₁)/(x₂−x₁) to find the gradient of an interval.

title: Apply the distance formula
blurb: Use d=√((x₂−x₁)²+(y₂−y₁)²), derived from Pythagoras, to find the distance between two points.

title: Convert between gradient–intercept and general form
blurb: Rearrange a line's equation between y=mx+c and ax+by+c=0.

title: Find a line's equation using point–gradient form
blurb: Use y−y₁=m(x−x₁) to find the equation of a line through a point with given gradient, or through two points.

title: Find and test parallel and perpendicular lines in any form
blurb: Find equations of, and test whether two lines are, parallel or perpendicular in any form.

title: Solve problems using coordinate geometry formulas
blurb: Apply midpoint, gradient and distance formulas to problems involving geometrical figures.

title: Identify line and rotational symmetry
blurb: Identify axes of symmetry and rotational symmetry in plane shapes and graphs.

title: Transform points on the Cartesian plane
blurb: Find coordinates of images under translation, reflection in an axis and rotation by multiples of 90° about the origin.

title: Graph parabolas and describe transformations
blurb: Compare y=kx², y=kx²+c, y=k(x−b)² and y=k(x−b)²+c and describe their transformations.

title: Find intercepts, axis of symmetry and vertex of a parabola
blurb: Find x- and y-intercepts algebraically, the axis of symmetry x=−b/2a and the vertex of y=ax²+bx+c.

title: Sketch parabolas from their equation
blurb: Graph y=ax²+bx+c using its features without graphing software.

title: Graph exponentials and describe transformations
blurb: Graph y=k(a)^x+c and y=k(a)^(−x)+c, finding asymptotes and intercepts.

title: Graph hyperbolas and describe transformations
blurb: Graph y=k/x, y=k/x+c and y=k/(x−b), identifying asymptotes and intercepts.

title: Graph circles centred at the origin
blurb: Derive and use x²+y²=r² for a circle with centre (0,0) and radius r.

title: Graph circles with centre (a,b)
blurb: Use (x−a)²+(y−b)²=r² and complete the square to find the centre and radius.

title: Identify graph types from their equations
blurb: Distinguish lines, parabolas, exponentials, hyperbolas and circles from their equations and key features.

title: Find intersections of a line and a curve
blurb: Find where a line meets a parabola, hyperbola or circle, both graphically and algebraically.

title: Graph and compare power curves
blurb: Graph y=kxⁿ (n≥2), describing the effect of odd vs even n and transformations y=kxⁿ+c and y=k(x−b)ⁿ.

title: Define and describe polynomials
blurb: Recognise polynomial expressions and describe degree, leading term/coefficient, constant term and monic/non-monic.

title: Use polynomial notation P(x)
blurb: Apply P(x) notation and evaluate P(c) for a given value of x.

title: Add, subtract and multiply polynomials
blurb: Perform the operations P(x)±Q(x) and P(x)×Q(x).

title: Divide a polynomial by a linear polynomial
blurb: Find the quotient and remainder and express P(x)=D(x)Q(x)+R(x).

title: Apply the remainder theorem
blurb: Verify and use the remainder theorem to find remainders and unknown coefficients.

title: Apply the factor theorem
blurb: Use the factor theorem to factorise polynomials and solve P(x)=0 (degree ≤4).

title: Graph polynomials from factored form
blurb: Graph quadratic, cubic and quartic polynomials by finding their zeroes.

title: Graph polynomials using roots and leading term
blurb: Use the sign of the leading term and the multiplicity of roots to graph y=P(x) and compare y=−P(x), y=P(−x), y=P(x)+c, y=kP(x).

title: Define a logarithm
blurb: Define log_a as the index to which base a is raised, and recognise y=aˣ ⇔ x=log_a y.

title: Translate between index and logarithmic form
blurb: Convert statements between index form and logarithmic form.

title: Compare exponential and logarithmic graphs
blurb: Graph y=aˣ and y=log_a x, noting symmetry in y=x and increasing/decreasing behaviour.

title: Apply the laws of logarithms
blurb: Deduce and use log_a(mn)=log_a m+log_a n, log_a(m/n)=log_a m−log_a n and log_a(mⁿ)=n·log_a m.

title: Use standard logarithmic results
blurb: Use log_a a=1, log_a 1=0, log_a(1/x)=−log_a x and the change-of-base rule.

title: Evaluate and simplify logarithmic expressions
blurb: Apply the log laws to evaluate and simplify expressions.

title: Solve exponential and logarithmic equations
blurb: Solve simple equations involving exponents or logarithms.

title: Interpret logarithmic scales
blurb: Explain logarithmic scales such as the Richter scale, decibels and f-stops.

title: Define relations and functions
blurb: Describe relations as associations between sets and functions as relations with one output per input.

title: Apply the vertical line test
blurb: Use the vertical line test to decide whether a graph represents a function.

title: Use function notation f(x)
blurb: Express functions using f(x) and evaluate f(c).

title: Find the domain and range of a function
blurb: Determine the domain and range of a variety of functions.

title: Graph transformations of functions
blurb: Graph and compare y=f(x), y=f(x)+c, y=f(x−b), y=kf(x) and y=f(ax).

title: Graph regions for linear inequalities
blurb: Graph linear inequalities such as ax+by>c by testing points and shading regions.

title: Apply Pythagoras' theorem in 3D
blurb: Find edge and diagonal lengths of rectangular prisms and other 3D objects.

title: Apply trigonometry in 3D
blurb: Solve right-angled triangle problems in 3 dimensions, including bearings and elevation/depression.

title: Apply the sine rule
blurb: Use a/sinA=b/sinB=c/sinC to find unknown sides and angles (ambiguous case excluded).

title: Apply the cosine rule
blurb: Use c²=a²+b²−2ab·cosC to find unknown sides, and rearrange to find unknown angles.

title: Apply the area rule for triangles
blurb: Use A=½ab·sinC to find the area of a triangle.

title: Solve non-right-angled triangle problems
blurb: Select and apply the sine, cosine or area rule to solve problems.

title: Define sine and cosine using the unit circle
blurb: Redefine the sine and cosine ratios in terms of coordinates on the unit circle.

title: Express tangent as sine over cosine
blurb: Verify that tanθ=sinθ/cosθ using the unit circle.

title: Graph trigonometric functions
blurb: Graph sine, cosine and tangent for angles of any magnitude and describe periodicity and symmetry.

title: Apply relationships for obtuse angles
blurb: Use sinA=sin(180°−A), cosA=−cos(180°−A) and tanA=−tan(180°−A).

title: Relate gradient to angle of inclination
blurb: Use m=tanθ, where θ is the angle a line makes with the x-axis.

title: Derive exact trigonometric ratios
blurb: Derive and apply exact sin, cos and tan for 30°, 45° and 60°.

title: Use complementary-angle relationships
blurb: Use sinA=cos(90°−A) and cosA=sin(90°−A).

title: Solve trigonometric equations
blurb: Find acute and obtuse angles for a given trig ratio and solve, including the ambiguous case of the sine rule.

title: Identify slant and perpendicular heights
blurb: Identify and find perpendicular and slant heights of right pyramids and cones using Pythagoras.

title: Find the surface area of a right pyramid
blurb: Find the surface area as the sum of the triangular faces and the base.

title: Find the surface area of a cone
blurb: Apply the curved surface area A=πrl for right cones.

title: Find the surface area of a sphere
blurb: Apply A=4πr² for spheres.

title: Solve surface area problems for composite solids
blurb: Find surface areas of composite solids involving pyramids, cones and spheres.

title: Find the volume of pyramids and cones
blurb: Apply V=⅓Ah for right pyramids and cones.

title: Find the volume of a sphere
blurb: Apply V=4/3·πr³ for spheres.

title: Solve volume problems for composite solids
blurb: Find volumes and capacities of composite solids involving pyramids, cones and spheres.

title: Identify congruent figures
blurb: Recognise congruent figures via translation, reflection and rotation, match sides and angles, and use the ≡ symbol.

title: Apply the congruent triangle tests
blurb: Establish and use SSS, SAS, AAS and RHS to identify congruent triangles.

title: Apply the similar triangle tests
blurb: Establish and use the four tests for similar triangles.

title: Apply area and volume ratios of similar figures
blurb: Use a similarity ratio a:b to relate areas (a²:b²) and volumes (a³:b³).

title: Find unknowns using geometrical reasoning
blurb: Apply geometrical facts and properties to find unknown sides and angles in plane shapes, with reasons.

title: Apply the exterior angle sum of a polygon
blurb: Establish and use that the exterior angles of a convex polygon sum to 360°.

title: Apply the interior angle sum of a polygon
blurb: Use (n−2)×180° for the interior angle sum and solve polygon problems.

title: Construct proofs using congruent triangles
blurb: Write formal proofs of triangle congruence preserving vertex order and draw conclusions.

title: Construct proofs using similar triangles
blurb: Write formal proofs of triangle similarity preserving vertex order.

title: Prove properties of triangles and quadrilaterals
blurb: Prove properties of isosceles/equilateral triangles and special quadrilaterals from their definitions.

title: Prove and apply tests for quadrilaterals
blurb: Prove and apply tests for parallelograms and rhombuses, solving Euclidean geometry problems.

title: Use circle angle terminology
blurb: Apply terms such as subtend, angle at the centre and angle at the circumference, and identify the arc an angle stands on.

title: Prove and apply chord properties of circles
blurb: Prove and use chord properties, such as the perpendicular from the centre bisecting a chord.

title: Prove and apply angle properties of circles
blurb: Prove and use the angle at the centre, angle in a semicircle, equal angles on the same arc and cyclic quadrilateral properties.

title: Find unknowns using chord and angle properties
blurb: Apply chord and angle properties of circles to find unknown angles and lengths.

title: Prove and apply tangent and secant properties
blurb: Prove and use tangent and secant properties, including the tangent–chord angle and intersecting chords/secants.

title: Find unknowns using tangent and secant properties
blurb: Apply tangent and secant properties to find unknown angles and lengths.

title: Describe networks and their elements
blurb: Describe networks (graphs) and identify vertices, edges and degree, recognising a graph can be drawn in different ways.

title: Identify planar and non-planar graphs
blurb: Define planar graphs, redraw graphs so no edges cross, and identify faces.

title: Apply Euler's formula for planar graphs
blurb: Use v−e+f=2 for connected planar graphs.

title: Identify Eulerian trails and circuits
blurb: Distinguish walk, trail, circuit, path and cycle, and identify Eulerian trails and circuits.

title: Plan and conduct a statistical inquiry
blurb: Design an aim and hypothesis, gather and organise data ethically, and report with data visualisations.

title: Evaluate sampling methods and sample size
blurb: Compare random, systematic, stratified and self-selected sampling and how each affects results.

title: Critically review surveys and media reports
blurb: Review surveys, polls and reports for accuracy and bias and examine how statistics shape decisions.

title: Represent data in Venn diagrams and 2-way tables
blurb: Construct and interpret Venn diagrams and 2-way tables and convert between them.

title: Use set notation for events
blurb: Use set language and notation A′, A∩B and A∪B and recognise mutually exclusive events.

title: Calculate conditional probabilities
blurb: Calculate probabilities where a condition restricts the sample space and interpret conditional language.

title: Describe mutually and non-mutually exclusive events
blurb: Distinguish mutually exclusive from non-mutually exclusive events and describe them using inclusive/exclusive or.

title: Calculate probabilities of compound events
blurb: Use Venn diagrams and 2-way tables to find probabilities of compound events, using 'at least', 'at most', 'not' and 'and'.
